CONCLUS IONS
1. Expenditures for your newsletter may be made from your
canpaign bairk account established for your current term of office,
so tong as your newsletter will not refer to your candidacy or
future eleclion and is not sent within three months prior to any
election for which you have filed a statement of intention to be a
candidate, a declaration of candidacy or nonination papers, or any
other documents necessary to be listed on the ballot as a
candj.date for any state or local office.
2. You may redesignate your carnpaign bank account
established for your cuirent tern of office as your campaign bank
account for your future electj.on. Comrnittees and accounts nay be
redesignated- even if the connittee and account continue to hold
campailn funds or outstanding debts from the previous election'
the- fuids may be carried over to the future election account'
3. when a single candidate sends a mass mailing, the
candidate's nane. sireet address, and city must be sho$'n on the
outside of each piece of nail in 6-point type.
D I SCUSSI ON
canpai.qn Bank Accounts
In June 1988, Proposition 73 was adopted by the voters- as
amendnents to the Act. Proposition zl provided that contributions
io candidates for elective office must comply with new fiscal year
contribution linits as set forth in Sections 85301, 85303 and
85305.3 In addition, Section 85201 provides that aII
contributions or loans urade to a candidate, or to the candidate's
controlled committee sha11 be deposited in a single carnpaign bank
account, and all campaign expenditures must be nade from the
appropriate carnpaign bank account.
Thus, pursuant to the Proposition 73 amendments to the Act'
a f tfroulfr i tandiaate nay have numerous campaign bank.accounts for
electiie offices, the cindidate rnust rnake alt expenditures
aisociated with a specific election from the appropriate canpaign
bank account established for that election. 
Moreover, Section
3 on september 25, L99o, in Service Emplovees.International
union. ail-clo. 
et aI. v. Fair Polj.tical Practices goBmissign
(lrro) ?4? f.S"pP. 580 ('tSEIUI') the United States Distrj'ct Court
int afiaat"a po.tiott" of the Act added by Proposition 73, including
aII the contiibution limitations in the Act based on fiscal years
(Sections 85301, 85302 and 85303) and the transfer ban of Section
dSgOS u" used to support the fiscal year contribution linitations.
This decision becarne final on January 18, 1991. Please note,
however, that the campaign bank account sections of Proposition 73
were not affected by the court's decision. (Sections 85200 -
a5202.) Iiloreover, the decision in SEIU is being appealed.


Our Fi■e No. I-9■-287
Page 3
85202(b) of Proposition 73 provides that contributions deposited
into the campaign account shall' be used only for expenses
associated with tne election of the candidate to the specj-f ic
office which the candidate intends to seek or expenses associated
with holding that office.
You have asked lrhether campaign funds raised to win election
to your current term of office may be used to prepare and
dislribute a constituent newsletter. Regulation 18525 provides:
(a) An incurnbent elected officer sha11 nake
expenditures for the following campaign exPenses
from the appropriate campaign bank account for
election to a future tern of office:
(1) Payments for fundraising and
carnpaign strategy expenses for election to a
future terrn of office.
(2) Payments for mass mailings,
political advertising, opinion polls or
lurveys, and other cornrnunications in
conneltion with election to a future term of
office. 
For purposes of this section, a nass
nailing, political 
advertisernent, opinion poIl
or survey, or other colflEunication shall be
considered 'rin connection with election to a
future term of off iceri if it nakes reference
to the officer's future election or status as
a candidate for a future tern of office, or if
it is nade by an incunbent officer within 3
nonths prior to an election for !,hich he or
she has filed anY of the following:
(A) A statement of intention to be
a candidate for a sPecific office,
pursuant to Governnent Code Section
a5200.
(B) A declaration of candidacY or
nomination PaPers, as sPecified in
chapter 5 (commencing with Section 6400)
of Division 5 of the Elections Code.
(c) AnY other documents necessary
to be listed on the ballot as a candidate
for anY state or loca1 office.
***
(b) An j.ncunbent elected officer uay nake
expenditures for purPoses not enumerated in
subdivision (a) from either the campaign bank

account established pursuant to Government Code
Section 85201 for election to the incurnbent terrn of
office or from a campaign bank account established
pursuant to Government Code Section 85201 for
ilection to a future term of office. 
This section
shall not be construed to Permit an incumbent
elected officer to make expenditures from any

asstciited with his or her election to the specific
office for which the account was established and
expenses associated wj-th holding that office'

As discussed above, a recent court decision invalidated the
transfer ban insofar as it was applied to limit transfers of
canpaign funds between a candidut",. orr,-"oniiof :,ea comrnittees. I

boti loca] and statewide speci.al elections. (section 85305')
l.loreover, section 85304 continues to prohj.bit Candidates for state

who is subiect to any valid contribution Iimits, includinq those

may redesignate a canpaign conmittee and campaign bank account
established after January 1, 1989, for reelection to the sane
office at a future date.
consequently, the canpaign bank account you used in your
carnpaign for election to your current terrn of office nay be
redesignated as a future election account. This would be
accomplished by filing a new candidate intention statement (Form
501) ior the future election, and by amending the existing
